site.title: Local Government Innovation Scan
site.tagline: A daily horizon scan of ideas and technologies reshaping Australian local government.

digests.posture: AI moved from single-council experiment toward sector infrastructure today: MAV and ALGA stood up a National AI Working Group while three Adelaide councils jointly bought a four-year AI partner — two different answers to the same question of how a 538-council sector builds AI capability it cannot build alone. Wollongong supplied the day's world-first, and Saturday's watchlist scan produced real yield.

Quiet Friday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.

Quiet midweek scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm, and 1 July brought the staged NSW and Victorian FOGO/kerbside-standardisation milestones, but these are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.

Quiet midweek scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) are all warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards already logged on 29 June.

Catch-up edition (7-day window). The week's clearest, most transferable innovation signal was the 2026 National Awards for Local Government (announced 23 June) — a deep bench of judged, replicable council projects spanning road safety, sustainable housing, resilient asset rebuilds, libraries and workforce — against a backdrop of active but mostly just-out-of-window waste/circular-economy moves.
